seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the viral sensation: homemade Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, perfectly golden and oozing with sweet and savory flavor!

description: Experience the viral sensation of Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, a delightful fusion of sweet and savory flavors. This beloved street food features soft, fluffy bread generously filled with a sweet cream cheese mixture and drenched in a rich, aromatic garlic butter sauce. Perfect as a snack or appetizer, it is an irresistible treat that is surprisingly easy to make at home.

ingredients.notes: like burger buns or specialty Korean garlic bread buns

instructions.title: Prepare the Bread
instructions.text: Slice each round bread roll into 6 or 8 equal wedges, cutting almost all the way through but leaving the bottom intact so the bread holds its shape. Set aside.

instructions.title: Make the Cream Cheese Filling
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, granulated sugar, and heavy cream. Mix well until smooth and creamy. Transfer the filling to a piping bag (or a Ziploc bag with a corner snipped off).

instructions.title: Prepare the Garlic Butter Sauce
instructions.text: In another bowl, whisk together the melted unsalted butter, minced garlic, mayonnaise, milk, chopped fresh parsley, beaten egg, and salt. If desired, add honey or condensed milk for a touch of sweetness. Mix thoroughly until all ingredients are well combined.

instructions.title: Assemble the Garlic Bread
instructions.text: Open up the sliced bread wedges slightly. Pipe the cream cheese filling generously into each cut. Dip the entire filled bread roll into the garlic butter sauce, ensuring all sides and crevices are thoroughly coated. Let any excess sauce drip off.

instructions.title: Bake the Bread
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Place the assembled bread rolls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown and the cream cheese filling is warm and slightly gooey.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Remove the Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread from the oven. Garnish with a sprinkle of fresh parsley if desired. Serve warm and enjoy the irresistible combination of sweet and savory flavors.

tips: For the best texture, use fresh, soft round bread rolls. Brioche buns or milk bread work wonderfully for a richer flavor.
Ensure your cream cheese is at room temperature for a smooth, lump-free filling that is easy to pipe.
Leftover garlic bread can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days, or refrigerated for up to 4 days. Reheat gently in an oven or air fryer for best results.
article:
Craving a culinary adventure that combines the best of sweet, savory, and utterly satisfying? Look no further than the viral sensation that has captivated foodies worldwide: Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread. This isn't your average garlic bread; it's a masterpiece of textures and flavors, a delightful fusion that elevates a simple snack into an unforgettable experience. Originating from the bustling streets and trendy cafes of South Korea, this bread has become a global phenomenon for good reason. Imagine a perfectly crusty bun, generously slathered with a sweet, tangy cream cheese filling, then drenched in an aromatic, buttery garlic sauce. If your mouth isn't watering yet, it soon will be. Get ready to transform your kitchen into a Korean bakery as we guide you through making this irresistible treat at home.

## What is Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread?

At its heart, Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is a delectable hybrid: part sweet pastry, part savory garlic bread, and entirely unique. Unlike traditional Western garlic bread, which often features a baguette sliced and spread with butter and garlic, the Korean version typically uses a soft, round milk bun or similar brioche-like bread. This bread is then deeply cut into six or eight wedges, but not all the way through, creating a "flower" shape. The magic truly begins with two distinct fillings. First, a generous piping of sweetened cream cheese is nestled into each crevice, offering a delightful counterpoint to the savory elements. Second, and perhaps the most iconic component, is the rich, emulsified garlic butter soak. This isn't just melted butter; it’s a fragrant concoction often featuring minced garlic, dried parsley, egg yolk, and sometimes a touch of condensed milk or sugar for a subtle sweetness and emulsification. The bread is then soaked in this mixture, ensuring every bite is infused with garlicky goodness before being baked to golden perfection. The result is a bread that is crispy on the outside, incredibly moist and tender on the inside, with pockets of creamy, tangy sweetness bursting through a savory, aromatic garlic crust. It's a truly unforgettable experience, and a prime example of innovative Korean baking.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread

To embark on your journey to create the ultimate Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, gather these essential ingredients:

For the Bread:
*   6 soft, round milk buns or brioche buns (about 3-4 inches in diameter each)

For the Cream Cheese Filling:
*   8 oz (226g) cream cheese, softened
*   1/4 cup granulated sugar
*   1 tablespoon milk (or heavy cream)
*   1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract (optional, but recommended)

For the Garlic Butter Soak:
*   1/2 cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, melted
*   1/4 cup mayonnaise (or full-fat milk for a lighter version)
*   3-4 cloves garlic, minced (or 1-2 tablespoons pre-minced garlic)
*   1 large egg yolk
*   1 tablespoon honey or condensed milk (optional, for sweetness and shine)
*   1 tablespoon dried parsley flakes
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   Pinch of black pepper

For Garnish:
*   Extra dried parsley flakes
*   Powdered sugar (optional)

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread

Follow these steps carefully to achieve bakery-quality Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread at home:

1.  **Prepare the Buns:** Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Using a sharp serrated knife, make 6-8 deep cuts into each bun, radiating from the center outwards, but do not cut all the way through the bottom. You want the cuts to be about 3/4 of the way through, creating pockets for the filling. Place the cut buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.

2.  **Make the Cream Cheese Filling:** In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, granulated sugar, milk, and vanilla extract (if using). Beat with an electric mixer or whisk until smooth, creamy, and lump-free. Transfer the cream cheese mixture to a piping bag fitted with a round tip (or a Ziploc bag with a corner snipped off).

3.  **Fill the Buns:** Carefully pipe the cream cheese filling into each of the cuts you made in the buns, ensuring each crevice is generously filled. You can also add a dollop of filling to the center of each bun if desired.

4.  **Prepare the Garlic Butter Soak:** In another bowl, whisk together the melted butter, mayonnaise (or milk), minced garlic, egg yolk, honey/condensed milk (if using), dried parsley flakes, salt, and pepper until well combined and emulsified. The mixture should be thick enough to coat the bread.

5.  **Soak the Buns:** Gently pick up each filled bun and submerge it into the garlic butter mixture, ensuring all sides and crevices are thoroughly coated. You can also use a spoon to generously pour the mixture over the top and into the cuts. Let any excess drip off, then return the soaked buns to the parchment-lined baking sheet.

6.  **Bake:** Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown, the cream cheese is slightly puffed, and the garlic butter is fragrant and bubbly. Keep an eye on them to prevent burning.

7.  **Garnish and Serve:** Once out of the oven, immediately sprinkle with extra dried parsley flakes. For an added touch of visual appeal and sweetness, you can dust lightly with powdered sugar. Serve your glorious Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread warm and enjoy!

## Pro Tips for the Best Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Results

Achieving that perfect balance of textures and flavors in your Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread comes down to a few key details:

*   **Choose the Right Bread:** The foundation is crucial. Look for soft, slightly sweet, round milk buns or brioche buns. These absorb the garlic butter beautifully and provide a tender contrast to the crispy exterior. Stale bread won't work as well.
*   **Soften Cream Cheese Properly:** Ensure your cream cheese is at room temperature before mixing. This prevents lumps and ensures a smooth, pipeable filling.
*   **Emulsify the Garlic Butter:** Don't skip the egg yolk and mayonnaise (or milk/condensed milk) in the garlic butter soak. They act as emulsifiers, helping the butter and other liquids combine into a stable, thick sauce that adheres better to the bread and prevents separation during baking. This is key to the signature moistness and flavor.
*   **Don't Be Shy with Garlic:** This is *garlic* bread, after all! Use fresh garlic for the best flavor, and don't be afraid to add a little extra if you're a garlic lover.
*   **Deep Cuts are Essential:** Make sure your cuts are deep enough to create substantial pockets for the cream cheese filling and allow the garlic butter to penetrate deeply, but not so deep that the bun falls apart.
*   **Bake Until Golden:** A perfectly golden-brown crust means a flavorful, slightly crispy exterior that contrasts wonderfully with the soft interior.

## Serving Suggestions for Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread

Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. It's fantastic on its own as a hearty snack or a unique breakfast pastry. Pair it with a cup of hot coffee or tea for a delightful morning treat. It also makes an excellent side dish for savory meals, complementing everything from a simple soup to a rich pasta dish. Some even enjoy it as a dessert due to its sweet cream cheese core. No matter how you choose to enjoy it, make sure it's warm for the ultimate experience.

## Variations and Substitutions for Your Garlic Bread

While the classic Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is perfect as is, there's always room for creative twists:

*   **Herbaceous Additions:** Incorporate fresh herbs like chives or finely chopped cilantro into the cream cheese filling or the garlic butter for an extra layer of freshness.
*   **Spicy Kick:** Add a pinch of red pepper flakes to the garlic butter for a subtle heat that complements the sweet and savory notes.
*   **Cheesy Upgrade:** Mix a tablespoon of grated Parmesan cheese into your garlic butter for an even richer, saltier profile. For the cream cheese filling, consider a small amount of mascarpone for extra richness.
*   **Sweet Sensations:** For an even sweeter dessert version, consider adding a touch more sugar or condensed milk to both the cream cheese and garlic butter. You could even experiment with a fruit jam swirl in the cream cheese.
*   **Different Breads:** While milk buns are traditional, you can experiment with other soft, round breads like brioche rolls, or even a soft dinner roll if you can't find specific milk buns. The key is a soft, absorbent texture.

Making Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread at home is a rewarding experience that yields a truly spectacular result. Its unique blend of crispy crust, tender crumb, sweet cream cheese, and savory garlic butter creates an unparalleled flavor profile that's both comforting and exciting. Dive into the world of Korean baking and savor every delicious bite of this trending delight!
